mission.title: Install and Configure Knative on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Serving is a Kubernetes-based platform that enables the deployment and serving of applications and functions as serverless containers. It allows for rapid deployment, automatic scaling, and advanced routing capabilities, making it ideal for modern cloud-native applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Knative Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add knative https://mesosphere.github.io/charts/staging
```

steps.title: Update the Helm repository
steps.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest version of the Knative chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Knative Serving
steps.description:
Install Knative Serving using Helm with the specified version:
```bash
helm install knative knative/knative --version 1.21.0 --namespace knative-serving --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Knative Serving components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Knative Serving components by editing the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment knative-controller --namespace knative-serving
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Knative Serving release using Helm:
```bash
helm uninstall knative --namespace knative-serving
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Knative:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep knative.dev | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Knative Serving namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace knative-serving
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Knative Serving configuration for backup:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace knative-serving -o yaml > knative-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Knative Serving
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Knative Serving to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade knative knative/knative --namespace knative-serving
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or node issues:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Knative services not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that the Knative ingress is correctly configured:
```bash
kubectl get ksvc --namespace knative-serving
```

troubleshooting.title: Controller pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the controller pod for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <controller-pod-name> --namespace knative-serving
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Knative Serving will have all components running in the knative-serving namespace, and you will be able to deploy and serve applications. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and that Helm and kubectl are installed on your machine.
